photo:
NEWS EDITORIAL IMAGE — one decisive moment that instantly identifies this news event and makes the reader stop scrolling. Do not retreat into vague mood shots, generic stock-photo scenes, posed models facing the camera, or smiling business people.
STYLE: reportage press photograph, natural available light, documentary realism, front-page news immediacy, unposed subjects caught mid-action — not a staged studio portrait or magazine editorial
SCENE: 굳게 다문 입으로 정면을 매섭게 쏘아보는 트럼프의 얼굴, 미국·나토기가 늘어선 앙카라 회의장 탁자 앞, 뒤편 대형 스크린엔 호르무즈 해상의 검은 연기가 번지는 늦은 오후 실내광.
HOOK (the idea this one image must convey — do not add elements beyond SCENE, never render these words as text): 트럼프가 이란 휴전을 '끝났다·시간 낭비'로 못박았다 — 다음 수는 협상이 아니라 폭격일 수 있다
MOOD (the reader's dominant emotion): 불안 1순위
CAMERA: low-angle shot looking up, imposing and dominant, towering presence, monumental, dramatic upward perspective, tight medium shot on the emotional core of the scene, a frozen split-second; choose an expressive viewpoint — three-quarter, profile, over-the-shoulder or a subtle height shift as the emotion demands, not a flat head-on composition (eye-level dignity does not mean flatness)
LIGHT: sinister under-lighting from below the face, inverted eerie shadows, menacing
STAGING (adapt this motif to the scene above, do not copy its literal props): two figures facing off at opposite edges, tense empty gap between them, eyelines locked, diagonal split into two camps
EXPRESSION & ACTION: brows lowered and drawn together, glaring eyes with tightened lids, lips pressed and tightened
FRAME: vertical 4:5, the scene fills the entire frame edge to edge — every corner is part of the location, no empty margins or bands; one clear protagonist with a single sharp focal point (eyes, hands, or a key object), background and foreground filled naturally; set in the event's actual country, region and people (this is a foreign-location news event); depict roles, professions and situations with generic faces.
AVOID: blank/white/black bands, letterbox or borders; watermark or logo; overlay text, captions, headlines or legible lettering (tiny blurred incidental signage only — readable Korean text renders broken); identifiable real individuals; sexualized or gratuitous depiction; any harm involving minors; graphic gore close-ups or a weapon aimed at the viewer (keep the dramatic tension, not shock).
webtoon:
REFERENCE FACE: the attached photo is the REAL face of the public figure this story is about. Redraw THAT exact person — same face, hairstyle and build, clearly recognizable as the same individual — in the illustration style described below. (If the attached photo shows no single clear human face — a building, chart, crowd or object — ignore it entirely and just follow the scene below. If the person is a private individual, a victim, or a minor, ignore this and keep them an anonymous generic figure.)

MOOD (the reader's dominant emotion): 불안 1순위
CAMERA: low-angle shot looking up, imposing and dominant, towering presence, monumental, dramatic upward perspective, tight upper-body framing, a frozen split-second; choose an expressive viewpoint — three-quarter, profile, over-the-shoulder or a subtle height shift as the emotion demands, not a flat head-on composition (eye-level dignity does not mean flatness)
LIGHT: sinister under-lighting from below the face, inverted eerie shadows, menacing, pushed to harder dramatic contrast

SUBJECT (who this scene is about — draw this public figure's REAL face, a faithful portrait of the actual person): 트럼프 대통령이 7월 8일 앙카라 나토 정상회의에서 이란과의 잠정 휴전을 "끝났다"고 선언했다 — 밤사이 미·이란이 상선 피격을 계기로 80여 곳·85곳을 주고받은 직후였다.
FRAME: vertical 4:5, the scene fills the entire frame edge to edge — every corner is part of the location, no empty margins or bands; one clear protagonist with a single sharp focal point (eyes, hands, or a key object), background and foreground filled naturally; set in the event's actual country, region and people (this is a foreign-location news event); the named public figure must look like the REAL person — reproduce their actual facial features, hairstyle and eyewear faithfully as seen in press photos, adapted to the drawing style (a portrait of that person, not a generic lookalike) — private individuals, victims and minors stay anonymous generic figures.
AVOID: blank/white/black bands, letterbox or borders; watermark or logo; overlay text, captions, headlines or legible lettering (tiny blurred incidental signage only — readable Korean text renders broken); photorealistic likeness of real individuals (stylized illustration likeness of public figures is allowed); private individuals, victims and minors as anonymous figures; sexualized or gratuitous depiction; any harm involving minors; graphic gore close-ups or a weapon aimed at the viewer (keep the dramatic tension, not shock).
